name: Calhoun Liberty
description_short: Member-owned credit union serving six Florida counties since 1957, offering checking, savings, loans, mortgages, and CDs with competitive rates and higher dividends than traditional banks.
description_long:
Calhoun Liberty Credit Union was established in 1957 as the Calhoun Liberty Teachers Credit Union to serve local educators, but has since expanded its mission to become a community-focused financial cooperative. Today, it serves nearly 10,000 members across Calhoun, Franklin, Gulf, Gadsden, Jackson, and Liberty counties in Florida. As a member-owned, not-for-profit cooperative, the institution operates under the cooperative business model where members are owners rather than customers of a shareholder-driven entity.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of retail financial products including checking and savings accounts, share certificates (CDs), personal loans, home equity lines of credit, mortgages, bill pay services, and debit cards. They provide online banking access and a mobile app for remote account management. The institution advertises competitive rates, including mortgage specials at 6.25% APR on 5/1 ARM products, home equity lines of credit at 5.99% introductory APR for six months, and nine-month CDs at 5.00% APY. They also offer financial calculators to help members estimate loan payments and investment returns.

Calhoun Liberty distinguishes itself through its deep community roots, emphasizing social responsibility and local reinvestment. The credit union holds a 5-Star Superior rating from independent agency Bauer Financial, indicating strong financial stability. As a cooperative, the institution returns profits to members through lower fees and higher dividends compared to commercial banks. The organization explicitly positions itself as "a family devoted to community betterment" and maintains a fraud prevention commitment.

The main limitation is geographic scope—membership is restricted to residents and employees in specific Florida counties, unlike national banks or credit unions. As a smaller institution with nearly 10,000 members, CLCU may have fewer branch locations and less extensive service infrastructure than large national competitors. The website does not clearly disclose all membership eligibility criteria, fee schedules, or competitive rate comparisons, which may require direct inquiry to evaluate total cost of ownership.
services: Checking accounts with online and mobile banking access
Share certificates (CDs) including IRA CD options
Home equity lines of credit with introductory rates
Financial calculators for loan payments and CD interest projections
Member service centers across six Florida counties
Fraud prevention and account security services
pros: 5-Star Superior rating from Bauer Financial, indicating strong financial stability and reserve ratios
Competitive mortgage rates advertised at 6.25% APR on 5/1 ARM products
Home equity line of credit introductory rate at 5.99% APR for six months
9-month CD earning 5.00% APY, competitive with current market rates
Member-owned cooperative structure returns profits through lower fees and higher dividends than traditional banks
Mobile app available on both iOS and Android for convenient account management
Online bill pay service integrated into digital banking platform
69-year operating history demonstrates institutional longevity and community commitment
cons: Geographic restriction: membership limited to six Florida counties (Calhoun, Franklin, Gulf, Gadsden, Jackson, Liberty), excluding most of Florida and other states
Limited branch network typical of smaller regional credit unions compared to national institutions
Website does not provide transparent fee schedule, APRs for personal loans, or detailed membership eligibility criteria
Smaller member base (nearly 10,000) may result in fewer service centers and less extensive product customization
No mention of overdraft protection, credit card products, or business lending services on primary website
best_for: Residents and employees of Calhoun, Franklin, Gulf, Gadsden, Jackson, and Liberty counties in Florida seeking member-owned financial institution benefits
Homebuyers in the service area looking for competitive mortgage rates and personalized loan underwriting
Conservative savers interested in NCUA-insured share certificates and higher dividend yields than commercial banks
Community-minded individuals prioritizing local financial institution reinvestment over large corporate banking structures
diagnosis: Calhoun Liberty Credit Union is best for individuals and families living or working in six specific Florida counties who value the member-owned cooperative model and want competitive rates on mortgages, CDs, and savings products. The main caveat is strict geographic eligibility—membership is limited to the six-county service area, making it unsuitable for consumers outside this region, and the institution's smaller size may limit service availability compared to regional or national alternatives.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Calhoun Liberty — Blountstown, FL Credit Union | CreditDoc
meta_description: Calhoun Liberty — NCUA-insured credit union in Blountstown, FL. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
